commit.message:
Fix SVG NaN crash on Stock Detail insights tab in demo mode

Root cause: GetStockAnalysis mock was missing required fields that
ConsumerSpendingSurgeChart and SmartMoneyFlowChart need to render:
- spendingData was missing spendingChange, priceChange, price fields
  → chart computed undefined * 100 = NaN → SVG path M64,NaN Q 70,NaN...
- optionsFlowData was missing price, unusualVolumePercent, sweepCount,
  putCallRatio fields → SmartMoneyFlowChart rendered NaN price data

Fixes:
- mockApolloClient.ts: add all missing fields to GetStockAnalysis mock
  (spendingChange/priceChange on spendingData; price/unusualVolumePercent/
   sweepCount/putCallRatio on optionsFlowData) with realistic values
- ConsumerSpendingSurgeChart.tsx: add isFinite() guard so missing/NaN
  values fall back to 0 instead of crashing the SVG renderer
- SmartMoneyFlowChart.tsx: add isFinite() guard on price data array

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Downgrade Redis URL parse failure from critical to warning in validate-env

Redis is labeled Optional in CHECK 7 but logError was used in the URL-parse
catch block, causing the server to refuse to start when the URL contains
base64 password characters (+, =, /) that are valid for ioredis but rejected
by the WHATWG URL parser (used by new URL()).

- Add redis(s):// scheme regex pre-check before calling new URL()
- Change catch logError → logWarning so an unparse-able Redis URL never
  blocks a production startup (ioredis handles these URLs correctly at runtime)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: XDP host-inbound-all drops unknown services, causing cluster split-brain

xdp_policy.c host-inbound fallback checked `hif != 0 && (flags & hif)`
but host_inbound_flag() returns 0 for unknown services (e.g. heartbeat
UDP 4784). When host_inbound_flags == HOST_INBOUND_ALL, this incorrectly
denied the packet. xdp_forward.c already had the correct check
(`flags != HOST_INBOUND_ALL`).

After `bpfrxd cleanup` during deploy, conntrack sessions are wiped.
New heartbeat packets hit xdp_policy as new flows, got denied by the
broken check, and both nodes declared peer lost — split-brain.

Also fixes isPeerInterface() to use FPC slot→node-id mapping for
cross-node monitor interface proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(core): add api_key parameter to web search opcodes (#45)

* feat(core): add client pattern to web search opcodes

Add web_search_create_client opcode that accepts an API key and returns
a TavilyClient, following the same pattern as HubSpot opcodes. All
web search opcodes now take client as first parameter, allowing
workflows to pass API keys from _env instead of requiring OS env vars.

Backward compatible: passing None as client falls back to TAVILY_API_KEY
environment variable.

- Improve error message clarity when no API key is found

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): make client param optional for backward compatibility

Move client parameter after query with None default on web_search,
web_search_news, and web_search_context opcodes. Existing callers
that don't pass a client now fall back to TAVILY_API_KEY env var.

Add missing tests: __repr__ masking, graceful degradation for
web_search_create_client, and env-var fallback for web_search_context.

commit.message:
fix(db,api): thread-safe all db reads; raise proxy timeout default to 600s (#31)

Closes #16, #17.

**Issue #16 — SQLite thread safety**
`check_same_thread=False` disables Python's ownership check but does NOT
serialize concurrent `execute()` calls on the shared `self._conn`. When two
FastAPI worker threads hit the same connection simultaneously, `sqlite3.Row`
cursor metadata and data tuples can desync → `IndexError: tuple index out of
range` on `dict(row)`. Fix: wrap every unlocked `conn = self._connect()` +
`conn.execute()` block in `with self._lock:`. 24 methods corrected in db.py.

**Issue #17 — proxy ReadTimeout floods logs**
- Raised `_timeout` default from 120 → 600s (matches `default_timeout_seconds`
  setting; callers may still override with `_timeout` in the body)
- Split `ReadTimeout` out of the generic `except Exception` handler and log at
  WARNING (not ERROR) — timeouts are expected for slow reasoning models like
  deepseek-r1:8b and should not pollute the error log
- Changed HTTP response from 502 → 504 for ReadTimeout (semantically correct)
- Updated docstrings and test assertion for the new default

535 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove preflight, fix picker popup, overhaul PDF design

Preflight module added no value — proof rendering fails on Vercel
(no pdftoppm), results written to ephemeral /tmp, never shown to
users. CLI command was standalone and unused. Full removal: module,
CLI command, config constant, web route refs, test mocks.

notification ("Photos selected! You can close the Google Photos tab")
when polling detects ready state and popup.close() fails. Auto-
dismisses after 8s with slide animation.

PDF design overhaul: removed all quote pages (5 fixed pages now),

* Add unified pipeline executor + dirty tracking (#266)

- New `pipeline.rs` module: `run_pipeline(state, from_step)` walks active
  pipeline entries and dispatches each stage (Rebin/Normalize sync,
  Analyze async via background thread).  `run_from_dirty()` convenience.
- `dirty_from: Option<GuidedStep>` in AppState with min-semantics
  (`mark_dirty` keeps earliest dirty stage).
- `GuidedStep` gets `#[repr(u8)]` discriminants for pipeline ordering.
- Studio mini-inspector: editable temperature DragValue calls `mark_dirty`,
  "Re-run from X" button calls `run_from_dirty`.
- `normalize_data` exposed as `pub(crate)` for pipeline executor.
- 7 unit tests: stage ordering, min-semantics, clear, invalidate, noop.

* Fix review P1s: pipeline re-run stale data + Transmission dispatch

- run_normalize: clear normalized/energies before recomputing so
  parameter edits in Studio actually take effect (cross-confirmed P1)

* Defer clear_dirty until async spatial map succeeds

Move state.clear_dirty() from run_pipeline (pre-launch) to
poll_pending_tasks (on Ok(result)).  On error or cancel, dirty
state is preserved so the user can re-run from Studio.

Fixes Copilot P1: async error left pipeline falsely "up to date".

* Fix Studio colorbar layout: pre-compute image height before horizontal

ui.horizontal() collapses available_height to one line, causing
prepare_image_painter to compute a tiny scale.  Pre-compute the
image display height using the parent's available dimensions, then
use allocate_ui_with_layout with that height for the image+colorbar
horizontal strip.

commit.message:
Fix video playback network error on TV by using shared OkHttpClient

StreamerPlayer was creating its own OkHttpClient without the OCSP-lenient
TrustManager from NetworkModule. On TV devices/emulators with clock drift,
SSL certificate validation fails with stale OCSP responses, causing all
video requests to fail with "Network error." Now uses NetworkModule.client
which already handles this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

[fix][publish] Normalise PGP_SECRET before ci-release to strip embedded newlines

When PGP_SECRET is base64-encoded without `-w0` (the Linux default wraps at
76 chars), GitHub Secrets stores it with embedded newlines. sbt-ci-release
then passes it through `echo $PGP_SECRET | base64 --decode`, which produces a
corrupted key ring and the error:

  java.io.IOException: secret key ring doesn't start with secret key tag: tag 0xffffffff

Adding a normalisation step that strips `\r\n` before the Publish step means
the fix works regardless of how the secret was originally generated, without
needing to regenerate and re-upload the GPG key.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EoWxtBxFEK5ZcgaeTT4df8 (#6)

[fix][publish] Normalise PGP_SECRET before ci-release to strip embedded newlines

When PGP_SECRET is base64-encoded without `-w0` (the Linux default wraps at
76 chars), GitHub Secrets stores it with embedded newlines. sbt-ci-release
then passes it through `echo $PGP_SECRET | base64 --decode`, which produces a
corrupted key ring and the error:

  java.io.IOException: secret key ring doesn't start with secret key tag: tag 0xffffffff

Adding a normalisation step that strips `\r\n` before the Publish step means
the fix works regardless of how the secret was originally generated, without
needing to regenerate and re-upload the GPG key.

Track in-flight task IDs in _executing_ids so the scheduler loop skips
tasks that are already running. Previously, since last_run_time only
updates after send_message() completes, the 15-second loop would
re-fire the same task while it was still executing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix orphaned Filled levels: use Gtc for counter orders, Alo for grid orders

Counter orders placed with Alo were rejected by the exchange when price
moved fast enough that the counter price crossed the spread. ParseOrderId
then threw, the catch swallowed it, and the level stayed Filled with 0
pending counters — permanently stuck.

Fix: PlaceLimitOrderAsync now takes postOnly (default true). Initial grid
orders keep Alo (always rest away from price, maker fees guaranteed).
Counter orders pass postOnly:false (Gtc) so they fill as taker if price
has moved through them rather than leaving the level orphaned.
